title: Capture human resolutions of guard hard-stops as queryable preventive records
context: Director directive (2026-06-19, recorded on ADR-003): when the guard hard-stops a thrashing agent and a human resolves it, we must record the resolution AND turn it into a rule that helps future runs avoid the same dead-end. Today the chain ends at a blocked -> paused_for_human task: a human can fix it, but nothing durable captures WHY the agent thrashed or HOW it was resolved, so a future similar task can repeat the same wall. This is the learning loop that closes the 'err toward stopping' posture — the value of stopping is only realized if the stop teaches the fleet something.

options.name: reuse-decision-grounding
options.rationale: At the human-resolution step, capture the resolution as a tagged decision record (cause + fix + preventive rule). It flows into the existing `sb query` grounding that task subagents already pull before starting, so future similar tasks see the precedent. Add only a small capture trigger (a resolve verb/step) at the paused_for_human boundary.
options.tradeoff: Leans on passive retrieval: the future task benefits only if its grounding query actually surfaces the relevant record. Reuses infrastructure already built (decision corpus, grounding, the blocked flow).

options.name: active-preventive-check
options.rationale: Build a new deterministic check (in the guard or planner) that matches a new task against a library of past failure signatures and warns or blocks pre-emptively.
options.tradeoff: Catches repeats even when grounding retrieval misses, but much heavier: needs a failure-signature model, matching logic, and its own false-positive tuning. Speculative before we have real failure data.

options.name: freeform-notes
options.rationale: The human jots a note on the resolved task; no structured record, no query path.
options.tradeoff: Lowest friction in the moment, but not queryable and never surfaces to future runs — fails the 'help future runs' half of the directive.

reasoning: The system already has the two halves this needs: a queryable decision corpus and a grounding step that feeds task subagents prior decisions before they start. The only missing piece is a capture trigger at the human-resolution point that writes a well-tagged resolution record (what failed, the fix, and a one-line preventive rule). That is lean, reuses what is built, and is consistent with how the system already propagates judgment forward. Escalate to an active pre-check (option 2) only if passive grounding demonstrably misses repeats once we have real failure data — do not build the heavy machinery on speculation.

steelman.strongest_case: Passive grounding is only as good as the future task's retrieval query; a deterministic signature match would catch a repeat the agent's own grounding might never surface — arguably the only way to truly *prevent* (not just inform) a repeat, which is what the directive literally asks for.

In the earliest phase, volume is tiny and the human resolving the stop is the same person who would read any future record; a quick note may capture nuance a structured form flattens, at zero tooling cost.

blast_radius: New capability in the oversight layer (not yet built; lands with the escalation/oversight work). Adds a small resolution-capture step at the existing blocked -> paused_for_human boundary plus a tagging convention; no change to the engine core or schemas (resolution records use the existing decision-record schema). Reversible/extensible: if passive grounding underperforms, layer the active check on top without undoing this.

research.action: Reviewed the existing blocked->paused_for_human flow (sb block, ADR-002 deny->blocked) and the task protocol's grounding step (`sb query`).
research.finding: The propagation path future->past already exists; only the capture trigger at resolution is missing.

evidence.result: queued under the oversight layer as a director-directed capability

feedback.note: Confirmed the lean shape: reuse the decision corpus + grounding loop rather than building active failure-signature matching up front. Active pre-check remains the documented fallback if passive grounding misses repeats. Approved for build with the oversight layer (not now).
